title: destroy command - remove worktree and branch
description: hoist destroy <branch> [--keep-branch] removes the worktree directory and optionally deletes the branch. Runs: git worktree remove <path>, then git branch -D <branch> unless --keep-branch. Removes entry from .hoist.json. Idempotent -- destroying already-removed worktree is a no-op.

title: reset command - reset worktree to base branch
description: hoist reset <branch> [--to=main] resets a worktree to a clean state for the next task. Runs: git -C <path> checkout <to> && git -C <path> pull --ff-only && git -C <path> checkout -B <branch> <to>. Fails if worktree has uncommitted changes (suggest --force to discard).

title: pr command - create GitHub PR from worktree branch
description: hoist pr <branch> --title '...' [--body '...'] [--base main] creates a PR. Pushes branch first: git -C <path> push -u origin <branch>. Then: gh pr create --head <branch> --base <base> --title ... --body .... Returns PR URL. Requires gh CLI to be installed and authenticated.

title: init command - register repo for worktree management
description: hoist init <repo-path> registers a repo. Creates .hoist.json in repo root with: repo path, worktree base dir (default .worktrees/), branch prefix (default agent/), list of managed worktrees. Validates repo-path is a git repo. Idempotent -- re-running updates config without losing tracked worktrees.

title: CLI scaffold with cobra
description: Set up cobra CLI framework with root command and subcommands: init, create, destroy, list, status, reset, pr, merge. Parse global flags: --dir (worktree base dir), --prefix (branch prefix, default agent/). No implementation yet, just the command structure with placeholder Run functions.

title: list and status commands
description: hoist list shows all managed worktrees (from .hoist.json cross-referenced with git worktree list). hoist status adds dirty/clean state per worktree: runs git -C <path> status --porcelain and reports modified/untracked counts. Output as table (name, branch, path, state).

title: merge command - fast-forward merge to target
description: hoist merge <branch> [--into=main] fast-forward merges a worktree branch. Runs: git checkout <into> && git merge --ff-only <branch> && git push. Fails if not fast-forwardable (suggest using pr instead). Optionally cleans up: destroy worktree after successful merge with --cleanup.

title: create command - make new worktree + branch
description: hoist create <branch> [--base=main] creates a git worktree. Runs: git worktree add <base-dir>/<sanitized-branch> -b <branch> <base>. Sanitizes branch name for directory (/ -> -). Records worktree in .hoist.json. Fails if branch already exists (suggest --force to reset). Fails if not in a hoist-initialized repo.
